title: lamium amplexicaule subsp. moluccellifolium
intro: Lamium amplexicaule subsp. moluccellifolium, also known as purple deadnettle or moluccella-leaved deadnettle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Lamium amplexicaule subsp. moluccellifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and can also be used as a tincture or added to creams and ointments for topical application.
